summary: The diff implements REQ-17 by replacing the dodge button with a drag-to-reposition mechanic. PokemonBattleSprite.tsx gains full mouse and touch drag support with boundary constraints. AttackAnimation.tsx checks positional distance at impact time to determine hit/miss. Battle.tsx wires player position state into both components. The implementation covers most acceptance criteria, but the dodge detection approach uses distance from center rather than projectile-sprite overlap, and drag bounds are not dynamically constrained to the player's side of the battlefield.

missing_requirements.requirement: The dodge detection uses positional overlap between the incoming attack projectile and the Pokemon sprite's current position to determine hit or miss.
missing_requirements.reason: AttackAnimation.tsx uses a simple Euclidean distance from center (dist >= DODGE_THRESHOLD of 45px) rather than actual positional overlap between the projectile bounding box and the Pokemon sprite's current position. This does not satisfy the AC specifying overlap-based collision detection.

missing_requirements.requirement: The player's Pokemon sprite is draggable to any position within the player's side of the battle field.
missing_requirements.reason: The drag bounds are hardcoded as static pixel offsets ({ minX: -120, maxX: 120, minY: -100, maxY: 60 }) with no evidence they are dynamically computed or constrained to the player's actual side of the rendered battlefield layout.

hallucinated_features.description: A 'Drag to dodge' hint UI element that auto-hides after 5 seconds via showDodgeHint state.
hallucinated_features.reason: The BuildRequest does not mention any onboarding hint or tooltip for the drag mechanic. This was added in Battle.tsx without being requested.

hallucinated_features.description: A dashed drag-hint ring visual indicator rendered on the player's Pokemon sprite.
hallucinated_features.reason: The BuildRequest does not specify any visual ring or outline on the draggable sprite. The file summary mentions a 'dashed drag-hint ring' which was not requested.
